reviews.review_description: I just want say, I’m one of the top 1000 in the world for all the rides I have. It’s a lot of fun to  ride a bike and time goes by so fast I would recommend vzfit and play to anyone, and believe it or not I hate to exercise I use to to ride 4 hours every day using vzplay, my only complaint is when I join the Facebook group, I was hacked only because I had a easy password so I recommend to use multi factor passwords. I was hit so hard it took 3 months to get all apps back.  I so glad I’m back riding again, and vz support haa been great in helping out more than I can say for meta

First off thank you for creating this game/activity.  I am a walker and hiker. I don't own a bike. This app help me to get in my steps with time flying while having fun. Loving the slide show option. 2 things I want to ask if you could add in. 

YouTube option. I have YouTube premium.  Not the other 2 selections. But I use my headphones to listen to music on my phone. So if not, that's ok. 

My other suggestion and request (on my knees begging please) if you could add hands free sensor. Meaning I don't have to hold the remote. Exercising with the trainers my wrist get tired holding the remotes/controllers.  I believe it is possible for games to recognize users hands movements. I exercise on other apps that doesn't need the controllers. They can recognize my hands. Could you please have an option for the program to recognize my hands for movements without using the controllers?  My arthritis in my wrists flare up bad some days that it limit me to use the program. I have to cut my timing short and unable to do challenges.
I use VZ Play while on my exercise bike several times a week. The variety of games allow me to get an excellent workout without ever getting bored. Highly recommend!

Amazing app!

Yes it renders images from google earth, that’s why it’s so awesome!!!

I have run down roads in Iceland, run mountain trails in Japan, air punched my way through the Australian outback and danced my way across Helsinki 

I’ve found the perfect setting for me is difficulty at level 6 on slide-show mode, no person view and to disable the turn markers and panorama markers.. and free-style, no equipment needed, this game is far far far better without a stationary bike, all you need is yourself, the hand tracking mode also works flawlessly.

Thanks for making such a brilliant app, it’s worth every single cent!
Awesome concept being able to work out while exploring google earth. Unfortunately a monthly subscription is needed which for me takes away any redeemable stars.
The program doesn’t have anything that supports people with disabilities no fancy chair to use or have a graph. None of that nature. It really needs one. ￼

reviews.review_title: Thanks I love it when it works
reviews.review_description: The past week when I get on the fit app it goes to inside a  warehouse and im unable to move or leave that area inside a warehouse please fix this immediately,

I bought a really good exercise bike and then had to find a way to make it entertaining. This oculus app is fabulous! It connects easily to my wahoo cadence device and I can ride along and watch the world go by. Decided to buy the annual subscription which also lets my husband set up his own profile. I like the smoothness of some of their virtual rides, and also like riding through Google maps generated areas. Customer support is very responsive via email!

Relies heavily on Google Maps for most of its options. This could be great if the interface were easier to use and the visuals felt smoother. There are only four truly immersive environments (two scenes, each with day and night modes), which look nice but don’t offer much depth. Overall, I found the experience frustrating, it wasn’t clear how to interact with the program, you just had to figure it out. Since they removed the game elements, it’s basically just riding a bike and collecting coins in a flattened-looking Google Maps world. I’ll be moving on to a different cycling game for my workouts.

developer_response.body: Hey there, sorry that the app didn't meet your expectations. The tutorial should be enough to get you started and riding. Was there a specific issue you were struggling with? We are always here to help at support@virzoom.com. Additionally, the VZfit facebook group is a great resource!

reviews.review_description: EZFit is pretty great, added a cadence sensor and my 20 year old stationary bike and haven’t looked back. Love riding threw places I haven’t been for awhile. Very impressed with the company, returned my email question next day. Quest 3 also improved the resolution and google maps is always upgrading. The app has also continued to improve. If you have a stationary bike sitting there not doing anything, this app is a no brainer!

I think the app in itself is great, the content is amazing. For me, personally, I had to cancel the subscription because it makes me nauseous. Probably is the movement part. If it wasn't for that, it would have been a great app when I need to get on my spinning bike
Been dope so far. Way better than staring at a wall. It’s not perfect , but it’s the best out here right now and price  hands down.
I’m absolutely obsessed with this app, I can find a favorite location and recreate my favorite rides with Google Maps and is so much fun I don’t even realize I’m actually exercising.
I have enjoyed using VZFit for some time. However the options of music accompaniment have changed, and I do not like the changes.

developer_response.body: Hey there, thanks for being a long time user!  We loved the old radio stations as well, unfortunately music licensing costs are incredibly expensive and we were in a situation of either raising the subscription price, or finding an alternative solution. We hope to continue to add to the in game sound track. Is there a genre you specifically miss?

reviews.review_title: Biking the world while working out!
reviews.review_description:
I have used a number of VR fitness apps over the last year and lost 34 pounds. I have a Cubii under desk elliptical that I use as a recumbent stationary bike. It has a cadence sensor, so it communicates to my Quest 3 VR headset.
I have discovered VZ-Fit. It has lots of different pre-made trips, all over the world. It uses Google maps and street view to allow you to travel anywhere that they have mapped. I have riden around Colorado, Paris France, London England, Glasgow Scotland, Canary Islands, New Zealand and Canada. I have completed a 147 mile tour of Yosemite National Park. The mountains are my favourite! 
It has lots of great music and allows you to stop any time and take HD photos or take videos of your ride while on the fly. They even provide trainers and different workout routines. VZ-fit is truly amazing! It is the best VR fitness app that I have used. 😊

Love this app so much. I got tired of mountain biking, and riding thru town. Had a couple falls, worried about injury, not young anymore. Got a stand for my Trek Marlin so I could get my cardio done indoors. Just watched TV while riding. 

Now I’m riding around my neighborhood, Russia, France, etc. I especially love riding down the main roads and interstate without worrying about getting hit lol. Thx!!!!! Prob going to try Fly here soon.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the great review! Glad that you are enjoying it!

reviews.review_description: I've used this for 2 years and the lack of development is showing. App crashes, stuck in loops on tracks, lost the ability to select music. I held out thinking they would develop it but it hasn't happened so I'm out. Such a shame because it's on the cusp of being great
After renewing my subscription they take out vzplay which is why i renewed. No refunds Screwed me over.

developer_response.body:
Hey there, 
Sorry for the confusion here. We did reach out to you with further instructions on accessing the VZplay games,  but never heard back.

reviews.review_title: Found a fitness game I can play seated
reviews.review_description:
Or standing. Great for my limited mobility days as arm movements and swinging my legs while seated also work to propel the platform.
And I want to thank the game developers for not placing me on a rooftop or floating in the air just to play. It’s caused me to quit far too many fitness games. I swear it’s like no one has ever heard of acrophobia. At least in Beat Saber I get to choose which environment to spawn in.

As to game play, the training was simple and I like the options for POV and slideshow or google view. I’ve found that if I play seated any of these options works, but standing slideshow is the only one that doesn’t result in nausea.

I like that I can use arm movements to propel as well as simple leg actions. I’m looking into getting a stationary bike to use as well.
So far it’s a winner. I’m signed up for a month to see if it’s something I can use more often.

I use my Schwinn IC4 with this app, and it pairs seamlessly with my bike. The settings work well, and I appreciate the head-steering option. While the radio feature is nice, I typically prefer using my headphones. Although I haven't explored the games yet, I've tried both the Google Earth rides and the virtual rides. The Google Earth rides were a disappointment—they can make you dizzy due to the jarring transitions between static frames, which are poorly stitched and low quality. It’d be much better if these rides were real virtual tours recorded from a bike, as this would offer a smoother experience without frequently shifting viewpoints. As they stand, these rides aren't useful to me.

However, the virtual rides are enjoyable and remind me of riding motorcycles and cars in Second Life, although Second Life has superior image quality. The downside is that there are only four virtual rides available, which might get repetitive soon.

If the company can't afford real recordings, they should focus on expanding the virtual rides, which are likely more cost-effective to create. Additionally, the particle effects during acceleration don’t adequately convey speed; the animation should also accelerate, with surroundings passing by more quickly.

Regarding the pricing, $11 per month feels steep for what's offered. In comparison, Supernatural charges $15 per month and provides a wide range of workouts with great music, coaches, and impressive visuals—too bad they don't include cycling.

I hope they keep on improving the app because it's really the best one I found so far.

After a year away, I have returned to VZFit. I moved and gave my Peloton to my son because I had no room for it and now I do, so he gave it back to me. I didn’t realise how much I missed riding to all the different countries and how it made exercise so easy for me. I’m 72 years old and try to stay as active as possible, but this program makes it real easy. Thank you VZfit . Your support is amazing!

Sandra Cumella

I’m a cardiac arrest survivor and I use this app everyday. I’m 64 and I started out riding maybe 4 miles a day with it but because it’s so much fun to visit new places, I now ride 12-16 miles a day! Using this app consistently I’ve improved my heart health from an EF rate of 28 to a now rate of over 55! That’s in the normal range . It’s like traveling anywhere you want to in the world! Such an awesome escape from reality. The app uses google for images so it’s only as smooth as google. I get very bad motion sickness but haven’t had any issues with VZFit. After almost 4 years I still use it as much as ever.

I bought my Quest 3, then VZ Fit, then my exercise bike and I haven’t looked back.
I jump on my bike every evening and have just finished cycling from John O’ Groats to Lands End (958 miles).  Having a route and an end goal, along with the Google Street View visuals,  has made riding an exercise bike a lot more enjoyable.  
I have made a list of rides in various locations that I will do.
For £8.99 per month this app is significantly cheaper than joining a gym.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the great review and great job on riding almost 1000 miles!

reviews.review_description:
Makes  exercise  more  interesting  bu
t is limited in  map options unless you buy more courses .  Or write reviews for bonus courses  to try anything  other then the basic demo version.
Works great with the wahoo cadence sensor and my indoor spin bike
I had a stroke in 2021. As a result I was no longer able to ride my bike which I did quite often. Thanks to VZfit now I can. It's great.

Pros:


•You can create your own rides anywhere in the world where Google mapping exists.


•VZFit can be relatively immersive if everything syncs and your route has good mapping.


Cons:


•The mapping image quality ranges from medium to low.


•If you don't have an FTMS device there can be a lot of friction using VZFit. On every use and not just the initial setup.


•The gradient in the headset/app and the resistance on your smart trainer may well not sync. This ruins much of the possible immersion.


•Almost all the routes I've ridden (a couple of dozen) have had me on the wrong side of the road, so you're cycling against the traffic. On a high-traffic route you can end up with a vehicle reversing away from you for your whole ride. This kills immersion.


•Some Google mapping on certain routes has you going from one side of the road to the other often. This can be quite disorienting despite my being a regular VR user since 2018.



If the main thing you want out of VR cycling is being able to create your own routes then this is the app for you. It's the USP of VZFit. I would say that if you don't have an FTMS-compatible smart trainer then the monthly subscription should be lower. More in line with the trial price. It's an inferior experience what with all the problems/hurdles it creates.



Control over mapping quality and consistency for bike riding usage is out of VZFit's hands. If AI could be used to stitch together the perfect imagery from what's on offer purely for the use of cycling then it's possible that a much better experience could be offered. As it is the chopping and changing of imagery on every route is very distracting and immersion killing.



I'll be trying free trials of some of the competition over the coming month and will come back and update this review accordingly.

Trial tutorial doesn't let you test your cadence sensor or Bluetooth fitness device. Gotta pay first to test. The street view looks awful. Slideshow mode is a joke and continuous mode is janky and annoying. Definitely not worth the subscription price.

developer_response.body: Thanks for trying it out, we just wanted to clarify that you can connect a device during the trial, before you pay. You cannot however attach a bike during the tutorial/intro that we offer for first time users to try the app before doing the free trial. If you are having trouble connecting your device and are on trial please reach out to support@virzoom.com and we can help.

reviews.review_description: Can't change radio anymore?! Only play pop music. Is this correct or am I doing it wrong?

I've never ridden a stationary bike until now. I'm quite a bit older and can't ride a normal bike outside. 
But this gets me on the bike and I've done the 9 Mile rides with the fake surroundings and I love it so far.
I'd really like to do more of the biking  in real places but it's quite distracting. You see everything in a second or two and it moves. If that could be fixed this would be the number one exercise for me. 
I'm partial to supernatural actually. ❤️😁

developer_response.body:
Hey there!
Thanks for taking a look at the app and glad you enjoyed it!  You may want to try slideshow mode to see if it's interesting for you. It removes the distortion, but also the sense of motion that the distortion creates.

reviews.review_description: You are guided through a 15 minute intro tutorial before they show you the subscription paywall. It should be made clear in the description this is a paid monthly sub and not a "free app." It's unfortunate because it looks like this is the only 'running' fitness app on the Quest store. The monthly subscription just feels unwarranted given that you're essentially looking at a 2d flat render of images from google earth.

developer_response.body:
Hey Andrew,
Sorry that the app is not free. The meta store page mentions the subscription and the free trial available. We do offer 10 minutes per day for free after the trial period if you do not subscribe. If you can point me to where the app says that it is completely free I would love to adjust that!  Feel free to reach out to us at support@virzoom.com

reviews.review_title: Auto Charges after free trial - caution
reviews.review_description: Did the free trial. Forgot to cancel auto payment and got charged $159 (Australian $) for a year. Yes I forgot to cancel but there is no way to cancel the full year and get the remaining $ back. Boring app and there is no reason you should pay this much when there are plenty of non subscription model apps out there. Warning to all users: Cancel auto payment. Dev does not understand that conversion rates exist in their reply.

developer_response.body: Hey Wendy,  Oculus should be able to help you get a full refund if you intended to cancel before the 14 day trial ended. If you have any trouble please reach out to us and we can try to help out.  The fact that is AUD makes more sense (sorry it doesn't say anywhere on our end where you are from) Thanks for giving it a try and let us know if we can help!

reviews.review_description: I really liked the premise for this exercise app…however the background being from Google Earth is a terrible idea. Exercising to make the cart move through the scenery is great, but spending any time looking at the pixelated background, constantly morphing around you…cars appearing and disappearing…warped images…is really unpleasant. I honestly can't believe people will pay for this.

developer_response.body:
Hey Falzon,

There is an option in game to turn it more into  a slide show style. This will remove the sense of motion, since the distortion actually causes that, but it will give you clear images to work out to while you travel.

Hey Falzon! Just checking in to see if you have any more questions

reviews.review_description: Hi! Please add a light membership with few more mins a month. I like the concept but it is too expensive annually compared to other games
Not my cup of tea considering I started with supernatural then decided to branch out and try other fitness apps. So far nothing else has come close so I will purchase yearly subscription for SN.

developer_response.body: Thanks for giving it a try!  Would love to hear some feedback on how we could provide an experience you would enjoy more.  If you want to share it with our team at support@virzoom.com.

reviews.review_description: I have cycled all my life but the roads have a lot of traffic now and I am getting older so VzFit caught my eye. I tried it and I was initially worried about how I handled turns because I would freak out and get off balance. I chose to stick with it and I got used to spinning around right turns. I NOW LOVE VzFIt!!!!!

I was so excited for this game at first I wanted to connect my spinning bike and ride around the world. To my disappointment I was only ever about to connect my bike one time and ride every other time after that the game keeps saying looking for a sender. Will not be purchasing the monthly.

developer_response.body:
Hey Rushin,
Sorry you are having trouble connecting and we would he happy to help if you reach out to support@virzoom.com.   I would recommend starting with rebooting the headset, and checking the battery in the sensor. Often they ship with low voltage.

reviews.review_description: Keep saying checking membership timeout pls restart app when I connect to my pedal sensor today . Works just fine before. My membership is still valid for a month. It works fine when I start without connecting Bluetooth device.

developer_response.body:
Hey CJWoo,
Just checking to make sure this is resolved.

Thanks!

reviews.review_description: Can't even set up an account. Get to the screen but won't let me fill it in.

developer_response.body: Hey Shannon, Sorry that you had this experience. If you can reach out to support@virzoom.com we can try to determine what the issue is and get it sorted for you.

reviews.review_description: Buggy. Lagged. Didn’t load the environment on my quest 2. This was the demo. Don’t think I’ll be purchasing.

developer_response.body:
Hey TylerD33,
Sorry you ran into this issue. If you can reach out to support@virzoom.com we can try to figure out what is going on with your headset.

reviews.review_description: How you swing your arms.

developer_response.body:
Hey JoeJoe,
Sorry you ran into this problem we would love to help figure out what's going on. If you can reach out to support@virzoom.com we can try to help.

reviews.review_title: It takes ten dollars a month
reviews.review_description: It socks

developer_response.body:
Hi Flying Cat,
We offer a 7 day free trial and also free time to play every day. If you are having trouble accessing the free content let us know support@virzoom.com

reviews.review_description: The only reason I signed up for the free trial, was a function that is not working. I tried many different locations as my point A and B, but all of them gave me various errors.

developer_response.body: Sorry you ran into this problem. You can create a route either in the game using an address or lat/lon or by uploading a map you have created on google my maps. There does need to be google street view data in the location you are trying to map.  If you reach out to support we would love to help trouble shoot this support@virzoom.com

reviews.review_description:
wish i'd read the other reviews first bc every review that isn't super hyping the app is saying exactly what i'm saying: it's not worth $9.99/month.

i was bored, but to be fair, i find stationary bikes and treadmills boring anyway.  it's kinda cool to be able to look at scenery more interesting than my living room, like the grand canyon, but i found the pace slow and the virtual instructor uninspiring. it may be more fun with a smart bike, but for how i use it, it's not worth the monthly subscription. 

which brings me to cancelling my subscription: extremely aggravating. account management is not intuitive AT ALL. the in-app instructions say to cancel your subscription "in the occulus app" -- i checked everywhere i could think of in occulus, in the occulus app on my phone, looked back at the vzfit app, even tried the vzfit web site -- there's no way to cancel the subscription from their web site. not at all user friendly. i ended up going to the occulus web site (not the app) to cancel my subscription. extremely aggravating.

reviews.review_title: Brilliant idea, technology not ready yet
reviews.review_description:
I really like the idea, and I've spent a few hours trying different rides with advices from the community (the facebook group is very active and people are very helpful).

Unfortunately the limitations that come with streaming Street View are creating a visual experience that does not allow me to feel really immersed in the game, regardless of the mode I am using.

I can still see myself using it occasionally for my workouts but not everyday, so I am not ready to go the subscribtion route for this product yet. I'll keep checking if the game gets better but since it's based on a Google service and not its own tech it's unlikely to improve dramatically in the near future, sadly. Too bad... I definitely would be ready to pay that kind of money for a better visual experience :(

I now have tried for two days to start working out.  I paid for the subscription and received confirmation.  The app was downloaded to both my pad as well as Oculus.  It will not allow me to accept the terms and start enjoying this experience.  Please help.

developer_response.body: Hey there, sorry you are having trouble connecting. If you reach out to support they can try to help you out. Support@virzoom.com

reviews.review_title: Profanity is my first name lol
reviews.review_description:
When I put my first name if Christine it kept telling me to remove the profanity. Crazy they have t fixed it yet. 
After putting in a abbreviated name the rides are pretty fun and the sensor works great and n my stationary bike

developer_response.body: Hey Christine, Sorry for the trouble!  The profanity filter issue is being worked on right now! We are trying to strike a balance of being family friendly and reducing the frustration people are feeling. Should have a solution soon. Thanks for the feedback, it's always appreciated.

reviews.review_description: The team was very responsive to help getting my account back up. I highly appreciate it!
Very well thought out app

reviews.review_title: Nice App. Needs More Virtual Rides
reviews.review_description:
Firstly, I have to say VZFit's profanity filter is ludicrous! It blocked my preferred name "Prometheus", and it wouldn't allow me to use the name of the city "Nassau" as part of the title of a ride I uploaded that was set in the Bahamas. Neither of those words are profane - at all! They need to seriously rethink their profanity filter.

Speaking of the Nassau ride, I uploaded it successfully, but when I tried to ride it, it didn't work. It just gave me a black screen with a trail on it, but I couldn't move on it.

Having said that, I generally enjoy the street view rides, but I feel the Virtual Rides are where this app really shines. Google's Street View is just not finished enough to be the basis for an app like this. It's nice to be able to see the real scenery, but I find it glitches out a lot.

Today I tried the Virtual Rides for the first time, and I enjoy these a lot more: they're based on real places (the first one is set in the Dordogne), but they are 3D cartoon representations, so I find them very fun and relaxing. Sadly, there is only the Dordogne ride so far (you get the option of a day or a night ride). If the app focused more on the Virtual Rides and if the developers junked the ridiculous profanity filter, I would give it 5 stars.

developer_response.body:
Hey Prometheus,
Glad you are enjoying the app! We are looking for a better solution on the profanity filter. For your very specific issues if you shoot a message to support@virzoom.com or check out the facebook group we can address those in an easier place to reply to.

reviews.review_description: I love the app but want to know if you need a Bluetooth cycle device to connect to the oculus?

developer_response.body:
Hey Yenz,
For standing mode you only need your headset. To ride with a bike attached you will either need a smart bike that connects automatically, or a bluetooth cadence sensor to add to a 'dumb' bike. These sensors usually go for about $20USD

reviews.review_description:
One of the things that makes older people like myself a bit nervous in vr world, is working out...well, all of it! But the customer service for this app is totally 5 star, making it accessible for more people. 
Thanks crew, you made my vr time even better x

Of course, there are a few sticking points that I could fault, but the app makes me want to get on my bike every day. What more could you ask for?
Speaking of which, how can you tell that you're cycling with VZfit? By the oncoming cyclists who don't say hello back :)
Like it but it seems limited on options for the price. I’ve enjoyed a few of the tours but the scenes are blurry and a little glitchy.

developer_response.body:
Hey Myschief,
We are limited by what google street view provides to us for images, but you do have the option to ride anywhere that there are images.

reviews.review_description: I also want to cancel subscription. Unable to find anything about canceling.

developer_response.body:
Hi gaylejanczarek, I am showing you as never having had a subscription with us. If you think you do have one you can reach out to support@virzoom.com and we can take a deeper look.  
6/13 - just following up to make sure this is all set gayle if you can let us know!

reviews.review_description: Excellent & timely help for the many questions I had getting started with Oculus and VZft.

This app is insane! Ive never seen anything like it. You are surrounded by true, real scenery.  Trying to describe it won't do it justice. Ride or use an VR exerboard to travel all over the world. On real roads! Ok ok real roads in VR! This company is a game changer in the world of VR. You can make your own rides and share them with the group who will very quickly become your family! You can chat while you work out in multi-player or go solo...And if gaming is your thing????? You get VZplay  with all of this. Tons of games that race and shoot and relax with casual..... ok breathe! There are too many amazing things about this company and what they have created.

Up to now I could only dream of escaping the blah of my basement stationary bike to enjoy the freedom and motion sensations of outdoor riding anywhere in the world. Love it!
Great customer support too!

developer_response.body:
Hey Tmmogirl!
Glad you are loving it and thanks for the wonderful review!

reviews.review_title: The application is so much fun! Furtherm
reviews.review_description:
The application is so much fun!
Furthermore, the support for members is very kind and powerful!

As a perpetually curious geography nerd who needs to lose weight I find it hard to imagine a more perfect app than this.  I just wish I had discovered it sooner - it would have made the last year with its suffocating Covid restrictions a lot easier to get through.  It’s done the impossible - got me out of bed every day this week before work to do a 10 mile stint on the exercise bike.  The array of possible locations - limited only by the extent of Google Street View - is dizzying.

There are limitations to the technology obviously.  The VR works best on continuous (eg terraced/high rise) buildings and/or wide open vistas.  Low-rise buildings and those with gaps in between get distorted, but it doesn’t stop you getting a genuine feel for the place, wherever it is, and the feeling of movement outside of comfort mode is total.  There is something magical about pressing the button to “start ride from A-B” and being teleported there in a jiffy.

This app is the sole reason I bought an Oculus, and so far I haven’t felt the need to buy any others.  There’s enough on here to keep me going for multiple lifetimes.  But I get the feeling there is resistance out there to the idea of an app with a monthly subscription.  Not having tried alternatives with a one-off charge it’s hard for me to say, but I’m guessing you wouldn’t get the level of support you get with this app (they resolved an issue I had - chiefly caused by my pressing the wrong button - within the hour on a Saturday afternoon, and are a pleasure to deal with).  You probably wouldn’t get the continuous development of the technology and I’m pretty sure you wouldn’t get unrestricted access to copyrighted imagery via Street View.

In short I’d credit this app with improving my mental and physical health massively and I highly recommend giving it a go.
